missing_ramp.title: Ramps — California Requirements
missing_ramp.description: Ramps along accessible routes must have a running slope no steeper than 1:12 and a minimum clear width of 48 inches between handrails.
missing_ramp.requirement: CBC requires a 48-inch minimum clear width for ramps (vs. federal 36-inch). Ramps must include edge protection on both sides.
missing_ramp.stricter_note: California requires 48-inch minimum ramp width between handrails, compared to the federal 36-inch minimum.

missing_handrail.title: Handrails on Ramps — California Requirements
missing_handrail.description: Ramps with a rise greater than 6 inches must have handrails on both sides complying with CBC Section 11B-505.
missing_handrail.requirement: Handrails must be 34–38 inches above the ramp surface and extend 12 inches beyond the top and bottom of the ramp run.

narrow_doorway.title: Door Clear Width — California Requirements
narrow_doorway.description: Doorways must provide a minimum clear opening width of 32 inches. Path-of-travel alteration triggers may require 36-inch minimum clear width.
narrow_doorway.requirement: When alterations trigger path-of-travel requirements, a 36-inch clear opening width may be required. Measured between the face of the door and the opposite stop at 90 degrees open.
narrow_doorway.stricter_note: California path-of-travel triggers may require 36-inch clear door width, exceeding the federal 32-inch minimum.

round_door_knob.title: Door Hardware — California Requirements
round_door_knob.description: Door hardware must be operable with one hand without tight grasping, pinching, or twisting of the wrist.
round_door_knob.requirement: Lever handles, push-type mechanisms, or U-shaped handles are compliant. Round knobs are prohibited on doors along accessible routes.

missing_accessible_parking_sign.title: Accessible Parking Signage — California Requirements
missing_accessible_parking_sign.description: Accessible parking spaces must be identified by signs with the ISA mounted at a minimum of 80 inches above the ground. Signs must display the applicable fine amount for illegal use.
missing_accessible_parking_sign.requirement: Signs must be 80 inches minimum above the ground (vs. federal 60 inches). Each sign must include the maximum fine amount for unauthorized parking (CVC 22507.8). Van-accessible spaces must include 'Van Accessible' designation.
missing_accessible_parking_sign.stricter_note: California requires 80-inch sign mounting height (vs. federal 60-inch) and mandatory display of the fine amount for illegal parking.

missing_parking_striping.title: Accessible Parking Access Aisle — California Requirements
missing_parking_striping.description: Access aisles must be marked with blue paint and include a blue International Symbol of Accessibility (ISA) painted on the pavement surface.
missing_parking_striping.requirement: Access aisles require blue-painted borders and a blue ISA ground marking on the pavement. The access aisle must be 60 inches wide minimum (96 inches for van-accessible).
missing_parking_striping.stricter_note: California requires blue paint on access aisle borders and a blue ISA marking on the pavement surface, which are not required federally.

missing_curb_cut.title: Curb Ramps — California Requirements
missing_curb_cut.description: Curb ramps must be provided wherever an accessible route crosses a curb. Detectable warning surfaces are required.
missing_curb_cut.requirement: Curb ramps must have a maximum running slope of 1:12, a minimum width of 48 inches, and detectable warning surfaces with truncated domes.

missing_tactile_signage.title: Raised Characters, Braille, and Geometric Symbols — California Requirements
missing_tactile_signage.description: Signs identifying rooms and spaces must include raised characters, Grade 2 Braille, and geometric symbols for restroom identification (triangle for men, circle for women, triangle overlapping circle for unisex).
missing_tactile_signage.requirement: Restroom signs must include geometric symbols: an equilateral triangle (point up) for men, a circle for women, and a triangle-within-circle for unisex facilities. Signs mounted 48–60 inches above floor on the latch side of the door.
missing_tactile_signage.stricter_note: California requires geometric symbols (triangle/circle) on restroom signage in addition to standard raised characters and Braille.

missing_grab_bars.title: Grab Bars in Toilet Rooms — California Requirements
missing_grab_bars.description: Grab bars must be provided on the side wall and rear wall closest to the toilet in accessible toilet compartments.
missing_grab_bars.requirement: Side grab bar: 42 inches minimum length. Rear grab bar: 36 inches minimum length. Mounted 33–36 inches above the finished floor.

blocked_accessible_route.title: Accessible Routes — California Requirements
blocked_accessible_route.description: Accessible routes must be continuous, unobstructed paths connecting all accessible elements. California's path-of-travel requirements impose a 20% spending trigger for alterations.
blocked_accessible_route.requirement: Minimum clear width of 36 inches (44 inches for corridors). When a building alteration is made, up to 20% of the total alteration cost must be spent on making the path of travel accessible.
blocked_accessible_route.stricter_note: California enforces a 20% path-of-travel spending trigger: when an area is altered, up to 20% of the alteration cost must be spent on accessible path-of-travel improvements.

no_accessible_entrance.title: Accessible Entrances — California Requirements
no_accessible_entrance.description: At least 60% of all public entrances must be accessible and on an accessible route.
no_accessible_entrance.requirement: Accessible entrances must be connected to accessible routes and identified with the ISA if not all entrances are accessible. Signage must direct users to the nearest accessible entrance.

step_only_entrance.title: Thresholds at Doorways — California Requirements
step_only_entrance.description: Thresholds at doorways must not exceed 1/2 inch in height. California's path-of-travel trigger applies when alterations are made.
step_only_entrance.requirement: Changes in level greater than 1/2 inch require a ramp or other accessible means. When alterations trigger path-of-travel requirements, step-only entrances must be remediated as part of the 20% spending obligation.
step_only_entrance.stricter_note: California's path-of-travel trigger for alterations requires step-only entrances to be addressed as part of the 20% alteration spending obligation.

protruding_objects.title: Protruding Objects — California Requirements
protruding_objects.description: Objects on walls or posts must not protrude more than 4 inches into circulation paths if mounted between 27 and 80 inches above the floor.
protruding_objects.requirement: Objects below 27 inches can protrude any amount. Objects above 80 inches are not restricted. Cane-detectable barriers are required beneath protruding objects.

inaccessible_counter.title: Sales and Service Counters — California Requirements
inaccessible_counter.description: A portion of the counter must be 36 inches maximum above the floor and 36 inches minimum in length, with knee and toe clearance provided.
inaccessible_counter.requirement: The lowered counter section must provide knee and toe clearance complying with CBC 11B-306. This is required in addition to the height and length requirements.
inaccessible_counter.stricter_note: California requires knee and toe clearance at lowered counter sections, which is not explicitly mandated under federal ADA standards.

missing_directional_signage.title: Directional and Informational Signs — California Requirements
missing_directional_signage.description: Signs providing directions to accessible features must include the International Symbol of Accessibility and be placed at decision points.
missing_directional_signage.requirement: Directional signage must be placed at decision points along accessible routes and must include the ISA where applicable.

steep_ramp.title: Ramp Slope — California Requirements
steep_ramp.description: Ramp running slope must not exceed 1:12 (8.33%). Cross slope must not exceed 1:48.
steep_ramp.requirement: Maximum running slope of 1:12. Rise for any ramp run must be 30 inches maximum. Landings required at top, bottom, and every 30 inches of rise.

missing_detectable_warnings.title: Detectable Warning Surfaces — California Requirements
missing_detectable_warnings.description: Truncated dome surfaces must be provided at curb ramps, transit platform edges, and hazardous vehicular areas. California specifically requires yellow-colored detectable warnings.
missing_detectable_warnings.requirement: Detectable warning surfaces must be yellow in color (federal ADA only requires visual contrast). Domes must be applied at curb ramps, hazardous vehicular areas, and transit platforms.
missing_detectable_warnings.stricter_note: California specifically requires yellow-colored detectable warning surfaces, whereas federal ADA only requires visual contrast with adjacent surfaces.

inaccessible_restroom.title: Toilet and Bathing Rooms — California Requirements
inaccessible_restroom.description: At least one of each type of toilet room must be accessible and on an accessible route. Doors must display geometric symbols identifying the restroom type.
inaccessible_restroom.requirement: Accessible restrooms must include 60-inch turning space, accessible fixtures, grab bars, and proper signage with geometric symbols (triangle for men, circle for women, overlapping triangle-circle for unisex) on the door.
inaccessible_restroom.stricter_note: California requires geometric symbols on restroom doors (triangle for men, circle for women) in addition to standard accessible signage.
